definitions: A substance or layer that underlies something, or on which some process occurs, in particular.
The surface or material on or from which an organism lives, grows, or obtains its nourishment.
A molecule on which an enzyme acts.
A layer of material to which a coating is applied.
The base material on which a semiconductor device is fabricated.
examples: The enzyme binds to its substrate.
Bacteria were grown on a nutrient substrate.
The paint adhered well to the substrate.
Silicon is a common substrate for microchips.
